Scenario.title: Leviathan Targeting Energy Sector Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Energy Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting energy sector infrastructure, leveraging OSINT, custom malware, and lateral movement to compromise industrial control systems and trigger physical disruptions.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Public Information Source, Process, Windows Registry, OS Process, File, Network Traffic, Domain Name Registrar, Windows Service, Application Log, File, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Open-Source Intelligence (OSINT) Gathering, Process Creation (Mechanism), Delivery Method, Application Log Content (Mechanism), Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism), OS API Execution (Mechanism), Process Injection, Service Execution, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Pass the Hash, File Modification (Mechanism), Process Creation (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker gathers information about energy sector vendors and infrastructure dependencies through publicly available sources, identifying potential targets for exploitation.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker develops custom payloads tailored for industrial control systems (ICS) using altered deployment tools, enabling covert execution on energy grid infrastructure.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker delivers phishing emails impersonating utility providers, tricking employees into opening malicious attachments that establish initial foothold in the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker exploits unpatched SCADA system interfaces by injecting malicious code into application logs, triggering unauthorized command execution on critical infrastructure.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker creates scheduled tasks in the Windows Registry to maintain persistent access using privileged account credentials, ensuring long-term control over compromised systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker leverages stolen admin tokens to escalate privileges, gaining access to restricted control systems managing energy distribution networks.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker injects malicious code into legitimate industrial monitoring processes to evade detection while maintaining stealthy operations.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker extracts credentials from compromised remote maintenance terminals by installing and executing malicious services to harvest sensitive authentication data.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps the ICS network topology by analyzing traffic patterns, identifying critical infrastructure nodes for further exploitation.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally between systems using harvested credentials, exploiting weak authentication mechanisms to access additional network segments.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker aggregates operational data from distributed energy management systems, preparing for exfiltration and potential sabotage.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker uses encrypted domain names to tunnel exfiltrated data through legitimate process creation mechanisms, avoiding traditional network monitoring.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-681: Exploitation of Improperly Controlled Hardware Security Identifiers
stage.description: The attacker manipulates circuit breaker controls by modifying configuration files, triggering cascading power outages to disrupt energy distribution.

Scenario.summary: A complex, multi-phase attack targeting energy infrastructure, combining OSINT, custom malware, and physical disruption techniques to compromise industrial control systems and cause widespread power outages.
